rationale: The cited study is used in the theory section to frame deployment as a broad construct and to justify focusing on one deployment dimension (innovation). It is also cited in the methods section as precedent for operationalizing product support assets via current and prior promotional investments, which is part of the core model. These uses go beyond a perfunctory literature-list mention and materially inform the citing paper's framing and empirical setup.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the resource-deployment perspective in Slotegraaf, Moorman, and Inman by carrying it into an acquisition setting. It extends that logic from market deployment returns to how acquirers' product capital shapes target selection and deployment and, in turn, long-term shareholder returns.
